name: Pade approximant recurrence relation
subsection_title: Padé approximations to the exponential function
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
Let $V_{lm}(z)$ denote the two-dimensional vector
\[
V_{lm}(z)=\begin{pmatrix} N_{lm}(z) \\ D_{lm}(z) \end{pmatrix},
\]
where $N_{lm}(z)$ and $D_{lm}(z)$ are the numerator and denominator, respectively, of the Padé approximant with indices $l,m$ (both non‑negative integers). The theorem provides a recurrence relation for these vectors along sub‑diagonals of the Padé table.

variables.name: V_{lm}(z)
variables.definition: two-dimensional vector whose first component is N_{lm}(z) and second component is D_{lm}(z), representing a Padé approximant

variables.name: l
variables.definition: non-negative integer, likely related to numerator or denominator degree in Padé table

variables.name: m
variables.definition: non-negative integer, likely related to numerator or denominator degree in Padé table

preamble: The context involves Padé approximants and their table representation. V_{lm}(z) denotes a vector containing the numerator N_{lm}(z) and denominator D_{lm}(z) of the Padé approximant with indices l and m. The theorem gives a recurrence relation between Padé vectors along sub-diagonals of the Padé table.
